##NAMES=Piemontite
##RRUFFID=R060905
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_(Al_2_Mn^3+^)[Si_2_O_7_][SiO_4_]O(OH)
##LOCALITY=Praborna mine, Aosta Valley, St. Marcel, Italy
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Elongated purple single crystals associated with albite and romeite, R060323
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R060905
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_1.82_Mn^2+^_0.12_)(Al_1.95_Mn^3+^_0.97_Fe^3+^_0.08_)[Si_2.00_O_7_][Si_1.00_O_4_]O(OH) ; = lighter phase, and the darker phase is albite.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.891 b: 5.6995 c: 10.228 alpha: 90 beta: 114.99 gamma: 90 volume: 469.72 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
